notes: Rewrites the backslash-matching regexes in escapeArgument (lib/util/escape.js) to avoid catastrophic backtracking, fixing the ReDoS.
Rewrites parameterCount (lib/types/urlencoded.js) to count '&' separators with an early exit once the limit is exceeded, instead of splitting the whole body, mitigating the DoS.

ground_truth_functions: parseValues; parseObject; combine; merge; markOverflow; isOverflow; getMaxIndex; setMaxIndex
notes: Enforces arrayLimit for bracket notation by tracking overflow objects via a side-channel (new markOverflow/isOverflow/getMaxIndex/setMaxIndex in utils.js) and converting oversized arrays to objects in combine/merge, so parse.js parseValues/parseObject no longer grow unbounded arrays.
Guards __proto__ and prototype objects (Object/Number/String/Array.prototype) inside setKey (index.js) to prevent prototype pollution from crafted argument names like --__proto__.x.

serialize (index.js) now validates name, value, path and domain against dedicated RFC 6265 character-set regexes instead of the loose fieldContent regex, preventing injection of extra cookie attributes via the name/path/domain fields.

RedirectableRequest.prototype._processResponse (index.js) now also drops the Cookie header (not just Authorization) when redirecting to a different host, preventing cross-domain leakage of confidential headers.
Replaces the greedy /\{.*\}/ brace test in micromatch.braces (index.js) with a linear hasBraces() scan (new helper), fixing the ReDoS; also adds isObject and reformats isEmptyString.

UNVERIFIED: advisory ID GHSA-gcfr-3v94-fvhp returns no results in OSV, the GitHub advisory API, or web search, so the fix commit could not be located or its diff inspected. Likely a mistyped or unpublished ID (note: the real recent rollup advisory is GHSA-mw96-cpmx-2vgc / CVE-2026-27606, a path-traversal issue in sanitizeFileName, which does not match the extracted manualChunks functions). Needs a corrected GHSA ID to fill.

extracted_functions: isLoopbackHost; isValidLocalAddress; buildProxy; getProxyForUrl; resolveProxy; shouldProxy
ground_truth_functions: setProxy; httpAdapter; dispatchRequest; sanitizeHeaderValue; shouldBypassProxy; parseNoProxyEntry; normalizeNoProxyHost; isLoopbackIPv4; isLoopbackHost
notes: Adds helpers/shouldBypassProxy.js that normalizes hostnames (strips trailing dots, unwraps IPv6 brackets, treats localhost/127.0.0.1/::1 as equivalent) before NO_PROXY matching; setProxy/httpAdapter (adapters/http.js) use it to fix the proxy-bypass SSRF. The same commit also adds helpers/sanitizeHeaderValue.js wired into dispatchRequest to strip invalid header characters.

Adds 'Cookie' to Retry.DEFAULT_REMOVE_HEADERS_ON_REDIRECT (util/retry.py) so the Cookie header is stripped on cross-host redirects. The change is to the Retry class constant; no method body is modified.

Adds 'Proxy-Authorization' to Retry.DEFAULT_REMOVE_HEADERS_ON_REDIRECT (util/retry.py) so it is stripped on cross-origin redirects. The change is to the Retry class constant; no method body is modified.
get_netrc_auth (src/requests/utils.py) now uses urlparse(url).hostname instead of manually splitting the netloc, preventing .netrc credential leakage from maliciously crafted URLs.

do_attr (src/jinja2/filters.py) now routes attribute access through environment.getattr (using getattr_static to avoid the item-lookup fallback) so the sandbox's is_safe_attribute checks apply, blocking the |attr('format') sandbox breakout. (Extracted 'do_reverse' is just the preceding diff-hunk label; the modified function is do_attr.)

NOTE: CSV ID GHSA-q34m-jh98-afg2 is invalid ('a' is not a valid GHSA char); this is a typo for GHSA-q34m-jh98-gwm2 (CVE-2024-49767). Fix commit 50cfeebc: MultiPartParser.parse (formparser.py) now tracks accumulated field size across data events and raises RequestEntityTooLarge, correctly enforcing max_form_memory_size for large non-file fields; receive_data (sansio/multipart.py) only gains a clarifying comment.

Adds _urllib3_request_context and HTTPAdapter._get_connection (src/requests/adapters.py) and calls it from send(), so connections are keyed by TLS settings; verify=False on the first request no longer persists to later verify=True requests reusing a pooled connection.

_load_pkcs7_certificates (src/cryptography/hazmat/backends/openssl/backend.py) returns an empty list when p7.d.sign is NULL, preventing the NULL-pointer dereference/segfault when loading a PKCS7 bundle with no signed certificates.

safe_join (src/werkzeug/security.py) additionally rejects filenames starting with '/', catching UNC-style paths (e.g. //server/share) that ntpath.isabs misses on Python < 3.11, fixing the Windows path-traversal issue.

drain_conn (src/urllib3/response.py) now drains redirect response bodies with decode_content=self._has_decoded_content, so a gzipped redirect body is not decompressed unless decoding was already initiated, restoring decompression-bomb safeguards for the streaming API on redirects.

PoolManager.urlopen (poolmanager.py) now compares header names case-insensitively (header.lower()) when stripping headers on cross-host redirects, and Retry.__init__ (util/retry.py) lowercases remove_headers_on_redirect, completing the incomplete case-sensitive fix for CVE-2018-20060 so Authorization is dropped regardless of case.
cms_transform_new (src/_imagingcms.c) replaces strcpy with strncpy bounded to 8 for mode_in/mode_out, fixing the buffer overflow; findLCMStype only has a comment cleanup.

Introduces remove_headers_on_redirect (default Authorization) on Retry (Retry.__init__ and Retry.new in util/retry.py) and strips those headers in PoolManager.urlopen (poolmanager.py) on cross-host redirects, fixing Authorization leakage on cross-origin redirects.

Core fix (CVE-2019-11324): ssl_wrap_socket (util/ssl_.py) no longer loads OS default CA certs when a custom ca_certs/ssl_context is supplied, so verification fails correctly. The referenced 1.24.2 commit also bundles unrelated changes: IPv6-SAN handling in contrib/pyopenssl.py (idna_encode hunk) and case-insensitive redirect header stripping (poolmanager.urlopen, retry.__init__).

validate_email (pydantic/networks.py) rejects inputs longer than MAX_EMAIL_LENGTH (2048), and _build_pretty_email_regex bounds the email group to {0,254}, together preventing the ReDoS from crafted email strings.

extracted_functions: call; getattr; getitem; is_safe_attribute; is_safe_callable; unsafe
notes: visit_FromImport (src/jinja2/compiler.py) escapes curly braces in the template name (position) before formatting it into the import error message that is compiled into an f-string, preventing execution of f-string syntax embedded in an attacker-controlled filename (sandbox breakout).

extracted_functions: handle_websocket_exception; parse_multipart_headers; parse_options_header; _parse_content_type; get_content_type; get_input_stream; get_content_length; is_resource_modified; make_chunk_iter; parse_multipart; _line_parse; _write; _find_terminator
notes: Threads a new max_form_parts limit (default 1000) through FormDataParser (__init__, _parse_multipart), MultiPartParser (__init__, parse) and MultipartDecoder (__init__) in formparser.py/sansio/multipart.py; MultipartDecoder.next_event raises RequestEntityTooLarge once the part count exceeds the limit, and Request.make_form_data_parser passes it through, mitigating the many-parts multipart DoS.

extracted_functions: _init_decoder; _decode; _flush_decoder; read; read1; readinto; readinto1; stream; read_chunked; from_httplib; enforce_content_length; close; drain_conn; __init__; _fp_read; _raw_read; _init_length; json
ground_truth_functions: decompress; __init__; has_unconsumed_tail; get; _decode; read; read1; stream; read_chunked
notes: Adds a max_length/output_buffer_limit parameter to the content decoders (DeflateDecoder/GzipDecoder/BrotliDecoder/ZstdDecoder/MultiDecoder decompress + new has_unconsumed_tail) and threads it through HTTPResponse._decode/read/read1/stream/read_chunked (src/urllib3/response.py), so streaming reads decompress only up to the requested amount, preventing decompression-bomb resource exhaustion.
